initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> Player RED countered with 55 ZUP. This is still within my acceptable range (up to 60 ZUP), but getting closer to my limit. I will propose 58 ZUP, trying to get the best deal possible without exceeding my budget. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 58 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Player RED, 55 ZUP is still a bit high for me. I can offer 58 ZUP. Is that acceptable? </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: Looks OK. All tags are present in the correct order and contain valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Proposing 58 ZUP is still within my 60 ZUP budget, maximizing the chance of acquiring X at the lowest possible cost.
3. Opponent plausibility: The opponent previously increased their asking price by 5 ZUP. Offering another 3 ZUP increase might be pushing it. They might be willing to settle around 53-55 ZUP. This proposal might be rejected. Actionable change: Consider proposing 55 ZUP to increase the likelihood of acceptance.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason>, <player answer>, and <newly proposed trade> are all consistent. The <message> accurately reflects the proposal.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is my second proposal, so I'm still within the limit of 3 proposals.
</critique>
